intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 (matrix.mtx.gz, barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz). Using scanpy, do standard QC, normalization, highly-variable genes, PCA, neighbors, Leiden clustering and a UMAP, then identify marker genes per cluster and show me a UMAP and a marker dotplot.

blocks.text: Good, the triplet is available. Now let me read the skill for single-cell processing to make sure I follow the standard approach:

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "description": "Standard scanpy processing for ONE scRNA-seq sample.", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + first-pass clustering (scanpy). Halt and present_plan before running on an unfamiliar dataset \u2014 the QC thresholds and the clustering resolution are dataset-dependent. Steps: sc.read_10x_mtx; calculate_qc_metrics; filter cells (n_genes>=200) & genes; filter pct_counts_mt; normalize_total(target_sum=1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; scale; pca; neighbors; leiden; umap; rank_genes_groups (wilcoxon) for markers. Plots: QC violin, HVG, UMAP colored by leiden, marker dotplot."}

blocks.text: Perfect. Let me now load the data and run a quick inspection before presenting the plan:

input.code:
import scanpy as sc
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Load the 10x triplet
prefix = 'GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1'
adata = sc.read_10x_mtx(
    f'{DATA_DIR}/{prefix}.matrix.mtx.gz',
    var_names_make_unique=True
)

# Add barcodes
barcodes = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/{prefix}.barcodes.tsv.gz', header=None, sep='\t')
adata.obs.index = barcodes[0].values

# Add feature names
features = pd.read_csv(f'{DATA_DIR}/{prefix}.features.tsv.gz', header=None, sep='\t')
adata.var.index = features[0].values
adata.var['gene_name'] = features[1].values

print(f"Loaded shape: {adata.shape}")
print(f"\nObs (cells):\n{adata.obs.head()}")
print(f"\nVar (genes) head:\n{adata.var.head()}")
print(f"\nData type: {adata.X.dtype}")
print(f"Data sparsity: {(adata.X.nnz / np.prod(adata.shape)):.2%} non-zero")
print(f"Value range: min={adata.X.data.min()}, max={adata.X.data.max()}")
